x-enumeration-literals.name: Borrower
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Borrower (Debtor): The primary party responsible for repaying the loan according to the repayment terms and conditions.

Comment:
Example:
A customer who takes a personal loan and makes monthly repayments.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Lender
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Lender (Creditor): The financial institution or entity that provided the loan and receives the repayment.

Comment:
Example:
A bank receiving mortgage repayments from a borrower.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Co-Borrower
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A person who shares responsibility for repaying the loan along with the primary borrower.

Comment:
Example:
Two spouses jointly repaying a home loan.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Guarantor
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A third party who agrees to repay the loan if the borrower fails to meet repayment obligations.

Comment:
Example:
A parent acting as guarantor for a student loan.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Payer
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Payer / Payment Initiator: The person or entity that actually initiates the repayment transaction.

Comment:
Example:
An employer deducting a loan installment from an employees salary.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Collection Agent
x-enumeration-literals.description:
An authorized entity responsible for collecting overdue repayments on behalf of the lender.

Comment:
Example:
A debt collection agency recovering missed loan payments.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Loan Servicer
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The organization responsible for managing loan administration, including repayment processing, statements, and account updates.

Comment:
Example:
A loan servicing company managing mortgage repayments for a bank.
